problem.instruction: Read the conversation in <A> and the lesson plans in <B>, and follow the directions.
problem.passage:
<A>
(Mr. Choi, a supervising teacher, is talking with his student teacher, Ms. Han, about her lesson plan.)

SupT: Ms. Han, I checked your lesson plan and found a couple of things that may help improve it.
ST: Oh, did I miss anything?
SupT: As you know, before you get to the main listening stage, we want students to recognize the purpose of listening, right?
ST: Yeah, and it sounds quite challenging. How can I do that?
SupT: You can try activating schemata. Making connections between personal experiences and learning can facilitate students' comprehension.
ST: Oh, I see.
SupT: And I recommend intensive listening. You know, authentic conversations have a lot of contractions. So, how about playing parts of a radio show focusing on particular language features?
ST: Good idea. Thank you. Is there anything else I missed?
SupT: Hmm, why don't you also try making some creative activities? Students can sing a song or chant, or they can record their own voice.
ST: Got it. I'll try to find some that are exciting.
SupT: Great. That's all I wanted to point out.
ST: Your suggestions are extremely helpful. I'll make some changes following your advice.
SupT: If you have any questions, don't hesitate to ask.
ST: I really appreciate your advice.

Note: SupT = supervising teacher, ST = student teacher

<B>
After the conversation, Ms. Han revised her lesson plan based on Mr. Choi's suggestions. Below are the original and modified lesson plans.

Original Lesson Plan
| Stage | Teaching & Learning Activities |
|-------|--------------------------------|
| Pre-listening | T shows the aim of the listening activity. / Tasks about what will happen to a person in a picture. / T engages Ss in small talk. |
| While-listening | Tasks Ss to listen to a story. / Tasks Ss to make inferences about the main topic of the story. / Tasks Ss to retell the story. |
| Post-listening | Tasks Ss to write a summary on the story. / Tasks Ss to present on their summaries. / T provides comments on Ss' presentations. |

Modified Lesson Plan
| Stage | Teaching & Learning Activities |
|-------|--------------------------------|
| Pre-listening | T presents the purpose of the listening activity. / Tasks Ss to predict what will happen to a person in a picture. / T engages Ss in small talk. |
| While-listening | Tasks Ss to listen to a story. / Tasks Ss to guess what the main topic of the story is. / Tasks Ss to do a gap-filling activity. |
| Post-listening | Tasks Ss to summarize the story. / Tasks Ss to act out assigned scenes from the story. / T provides feedback on Ss' performances. |

Note: T = teacher, Ss = students

Identify the supervising teacher's TWO suggestions from <A> that are reflected in the modified lesson plan in <B>. Then, explain your answers, respectively, with evidence from <B>.

steps.label: 범위 설정 — SupT의 3가지 제안 확인
steps.explanation:
<A>에서 SupT가 제안한 3가지:
① Activating schemata: 개인 경험과 학습을 연결하여 이해 촉진
② Intensive listening: 특정 언어 특징에 집중하는 청취 활동 (gap-filling, 라디오 쇼 활용)
③ Creative activities: 노래·챈트·녹음 등 참여적·창의적 활동
<B>에서 Original→Modified 변경 사항을 위 제안과 대조하여 반영된 두 개를 찾는 과제.

steps.label: 이론↔지문 대조 — 각 단계 변경 사항 확인
steps.explanation: Pre-listening: 'shows the aim' → 'presents the purpose' (표현 수정), picture 활동 유지. Activating schemata가 반영되었다고 보기 어려움 — original에도 picture 예측이 있으므로 변화 미미. While-listening: 'retell the story' → 'gap-filling activity' — 언어 특징 집중 활동으로 변경. Post-listening: 'present on summaries' → 'act out assigned scenes' — 역할극으로 변경.

steps.label: Intensive listening 반영 확인
steps.explanation: <A>에서 SupT: 축약형 등 particular language features에 집중하는 intensive listening 권고. <B> Modified While-listening: original의 'retell the story'(내용 재현)가 'gap-filling activity'(특정 언어 항목 집중)로 교체됨. Gap-filling은 특정 어휘·음운·문법 항목을 집중하여 듣는 intensive listening의 대표 활동이다.

steps.label: Creative activities 반영 확인
steps.explanation: <A>에서 SupT: 노래·챈트·녹음 등 creative activities 권고. <B> Modified Post-listening: original의 'present on their summaries'(요약 발표)가 'act out assigned scenes from the story'(역할극)로 교체됨. 역할극은 학습자가 창의적·참여적으로 언어를 사용하는 creative activities에 해당한다.

steps.label: 전략 메모 — Activating schemata 소거
steps.explanation: 함정 주의: Activating schemata도 SupT의 제안이지만, <B>의 modified plan에서 명확한 변화가 없다. Pre-listening 'predict what will happen to a person in a picture'는 original의 'Tasks about what will happen to a person in a picture'와 실질적 차이가 없음. Activating schemata가 반영되었다고 볼 증거가 불충분하므로 소거. '반영된' 두 가지는 intensive listening + creative activities.

key_terms.definition_en: A focused listening approach in which learners attend closely to specific linguistic features — such as sounds, contractions, or grammar — in a spoken text.
key_terms.definition_ko: 발음, 축약형, 문법 등 특정 언어 특징에 의식적으로 집중하며 듣는 청취 방식.
key_terms.exam_usage: While-listening에서 gap-filling activity로 반영 — particular language features 집중

key_terms.definition_en: Post-listening activities — such as singing, chanting, role-playing, or recording — that encourage learners to engage creatively and interactively with language.
key_terms.definition_ko: 노래·챈트·역할극·녹음 등 학습자가 창의적·참여적으로 언어를 사용하도록 유도하는 듣기 후 활동.
key_terms.exam_usage: Post-listening에서 act out assigned scenes — 역할극으로 creative activities 반영

model_answer.essay_content: Intensive listening and creative activities are reflected in the modified plan. First, in the while-listening stage, the original story-retelling task is replaced with a gap-filling activity, which focuses on particular language features as suggested. Second, in the post-listening stage, students act out assigned scenes from the story instead of presenting summaries, incorporating creative and engaging activities.

scoring_criteria.criterion: 근거: Post-listening에서 act out assigned scenes 언급

variants.label: 답안 A
variants.content: Intensive listening: In the while-listening stage, students complete a gap-filling activity, focusing on specific language features, as suggested. Creative activities: In the post-listening stage, students act out scenes from the story, making the lesson more engaging and interactive.

references.author: Brown, H. D.
references.title: Teaching by Principles: An Interactive Approach to Language Pedagogy (3rd ed.)
references.excerpt: Intensive listening focuses learners on small units of spoken language — sounds, words, intonation, grammatical forms — often through tasks such as gap-filling or dictation. In contrast to extensive listening, intensive listening asks learners to pay close attention to particular language features. Creative activities such as songs, chants, role plays, and drama capitalize on the affective and kinesthetic dimensions of language learning, enhancing motivation and retention.

references.author: Vandergrift, L. & Goh, C.
references.title: Teaching and Learning Second Language Listening: Metacognition in Action
references.excerpt: While-listening tasks should be purposeful and appropriately focused. Intensive listening tasks — gap-fills, transcript verification, feature-spotting — train learners to process form as well as meaning. At the post-listening stage, creative activities encourage learners to personalize and extend what they have heard, supporting deeper processing and long-term retention.

source.concept_explanation:
청취 수업의 3단계 구조:

- Pre-listening: 배경 지식 활성화(activating schemata), 목적 제시, 예측 활동
- While-listening: 본 청취 과제 수행
  - Intensive listening: 특정 언어 특징(발음·축약형·문법)에 집중. gap-filling이 대표 활동.
  - Extensive listening: 전반적 이해에 집중
- Post-listening: 청취 내용을 바탕으로 한 확장 활동
  - Creative activities: 노래·챈트·역할극(act out)·녹음 등 창의적·참여적 언어 사용

본 문제 핵심:
- Intensive listening → While-listening의 gap-filling으로 반영
- Creative activities → Post-listening의 act out assigned scenes으로 반영
- Activating schemata → Pre-listening 변화가 미미하여 명확히 반영되지 않음 → 소거
